center.name: Cottonwood Sprouts Early Learning
center.tagline: A warm, play-based start in the heart of Albuquerque.
center.hours: Monday through Friday, 7:00 AM to 6:00 PM

sections.title: Hours & Closures
sections.body:
We are open Monday through Friday, 7:00 AM to 6:00 PM. We are closed on weekends.

We observe these holiday closures each year: New Year's Day, Memorial Day, Juneteenth (June 19), Independence Day, Labor Day, Thanksgiving (both Thursday and the Friday after), Christmas Eve, and Christmas Day. We also close for two staff in-service days each year, announced at least four weeks ahead in the app.

We remain OPEN with normal hours on Veterans Day, Presidents' Day, Indigenous Peoples' Day, and Halloween. When a holiday falls on a weekend, the closure is observed on the nearest weekday and posted in advance.

sections.title: Tuition & Fees
sections.body:
Tuition is billed monthly and covers all meals and snacks. Current full-time rates:
- Infant room (6 weeks to 18 months): $1,485 per month
- Toddler room (18 months to 3 years): $1,215 per month
- Preschool room (3 to 5 years): $995 per month

A part-time schedule (three days per week) is available at 70% of the full-time rate. There is a one-time annual registration fee of $150 per child, and a 10% sibling discount for the younger child. Tuition is due on the 1st of each month; a $35 late fee applies after the 5th. A two-week tuition deposit is collected at enrollment and applied to your final month.

sections.title: Illness & Exclusion Policy
sections.body:
To keep every child healthy, please keep your child home if they have any of the following, and for at least 24 hours after it resolves:
- A fever of 100.4°F or higher. Children may return once they are fever-free for 24 hours WITHOUT fever-reducing medicine such as acetaminophen or ibuprofen.
- Vomiting or diarrhea, two or more times in the prior 24 hours.
- An undiagnosed rash, or eyes that are red with discharge (possible conjunctivitis), until cleared or treated for 24 hours.
- A persistent cough or difficulty breathing.

Our teachers care for your child but are not able to diagnose illness or advise on medication or doses. When you are unsure whether to bring your child in, please call us at (505) 555-0142 and ask for Director Maria Delgado, or check with your pediatrician. If your child has a medical emergency, call 911 first. We are a nut-free facility.

sections.title: Meals & Snacks
sections.body:
All meals are included in tuition and prepared on site: a morning snack, a hot lunch, and an afternoon snack each day. We are a nut-free facility and can accommodate documented allergies and dietary needs with a form on file. The weekly lunch menu is posted in the app every Sunday.

Families do not need to pack lunch. If you ever forget something your child needs, just let a teacher know; we will provide that day's lunch and snacks at no extra charge. Please send a labeled water bottle and a change of clothes.

sections.title: Drop-off & Pick-up
sections.body:
Drop-off is between 7:00 and 9:00 AM so children can settle into the morning routine; please call ahead if you will arrive later. Pick-up is by 6:00 PM. A late pick-up fee of $1 per minute after 6:00 PM helps cover staff time.

For safety, only adults on your authorized pick-up list may collect your child, and we ask for a photo ID until our staff know them. You can update authorized adults anytime in the app. Please sign your child in and out each day.

sections.title: Tours & Enrollment
sections.body:
We love showing families around. Tours are by appointment on Tuesdays and Thursdays at 9:30 AM or 4:00 PM, and last about 30 minutes. Request a tour through the form on our website or by calling (505) 555-0142.

Enrollment is rolling, based on openings by age group; when a room is full we keep a waitlist. To enroll, we will need your child's immunization record, an emergency contact form, and the registration fee and deposit.

sections.title: Communication
sections.body: Daily updates, photos, meals, naps, and reminders are shared through the Brightwheel app. Parent-teacher conferences are offered each fall and spring, and you are always welcome to ask for a check-in. For anything urgent during the day, call the front desk at (505) 555-0142.

sections.title: Safety & Emergencies
sections.body: Our entrances are secured and every classroom is staffed within state ratios. All teachers are trained in pediatric CPR and first aid, and we run monthly safety drills. In any emergency, staff call 911 first and then contact families. If your child has a known medical condition, please make sure their care plan is on file.

sections.title: Our Program & Philosophy
sections.body: Cottonwood Sprouts is a play-based early learning program for children 6 weeks to 5 years. We believe young children learn best through guided play, hands-on exploration, and warm relationships. Our classrooms use a developmentally appropriate approach aligned with the New Mexico Early Learning Guidelines, and we help older children build the skills for a confident transition to kindergarten. Teachers observe and document each child's growth and share it with families through the Brightwheel app and at conferences.

sections.title: Daily Schedule
sections.body: Each classroom follows a predictable daily rhythm so children feel secure: arrival and free play, morning circle, a teacher-guided activity, morning snack, outdoor play, hot lunch, rest or nap, an afternoon activity or learning centers, afternoon snack, more active play, and quiet activities until pickup. Infant and toddler routines follow each child's own pace for feeding and sleeping. Exact times are posted in each room and shared in the app.

sections.title: Naptime & Rest
sections.body: All children have a daily rest period after lunch. Each child has their own cot or crib, and bedding is sent home weekly to be washed. Infants are always placed on their backs to sleep, alone in a crib, following safe-sleep guidelines. Children who do not sleep rest quietly with a book or a soft activity; we never force a child to sleep. You may send a small, labeled comfort item if it helps your child settle.

sections.title: Guidance & Discipline
sections.body: We use positive guidance to help children learn self-control and kindness. Teachers set clear, age-appropriate expectations, redirect behavior, model problem-solving, and help children name and manage their feelings. We never use physical punishment, isolation, withholding of food, or shaming of any kind. For ongoing concerns we partner with you on a consistent plan and keep you informed. Biting is common in young children; if it happens we keep everyone safe, support both children, and work with families on next steps.

sections.title: Toilet Learning & Diapering
sections.body: We follow each child's readiness and your guidance on toilet learning, and we keep it positive and pressure-free. Diapers and pull-ups are checked and changed on a regular schedule and whenever needed. Please keep a supply of diapers, wipes, and at least two full changes of labeled clothing in your child's cubby, and we will let you know in the app when supplies run low.

sections.title: What to Bring & Items From Home
sections.body: Please send a labeled water bottle, a complete change of clothes (more for younger children), and weather-appropriate outerwear each day, all labeled with your child's name. We provide all meals, snacks, and learning materials. Please leave toys, jewelry, and personal devices at home so they are not lost or broken, unless a teacher asks for a specific item for a class activity. A small labeled comfort item for rest time is welcome.

sections.title: Sunscreen & Outdoor Play
sections.body: Children play outdoors every day the weather allows, because active outdoor time is part of healthy development. Please dress your child for the weather, including a coat, hat, and closed-toe shoes in winter. Apply sunscreen before drop-off in sunny months; with a signed authorization on file we will reapply a sunscreen you provide. On days of extreme heat, cold, or poor air quality we move active play indoors.

sections.title: Birthdays & Celebrations
sections.body: We love to celebrate birthdays. Because we are a nut-free facility and care for children with allergies, please arrange any shared treat with your child's teacher in advance; store-bought items with a visible ingredient label are required, and non-food celebrations such as a special book for the class are always welcome. We recognize a range of cultural and family traditions and keep celebrations simple and inclusive.

sections.title: Attendance & Absences
sections.body: Please let us know through the Brightwheel app or by calling (505) 555-0142 when your child will be absent or arriving late, ideally by 9:00 AM. Regular attendance helps your child settle and benefit from the program. If your child is out with a reportable illness, please tell us so we can notify other families as required while keeping your child's identity private. Tuition holds your child's spot and is due whether or not they attend.

sections.title: Medication
sections.body: Our teachers cannot diagnose illness or decide on medicine or doses. We can give medication only with a completed, signed medication authorization on file, in the original labeled container, and following the label or a provider's written instructions. For any dosing question, please contact your pediatrician. If your child has a known condition such as asthma or a severe allergy, please give us an up-to-date care or action plan.

sections.title: Inclement Weather & Closures
sections.body: If we must delay opening, close early, or close for the day due to snow, severe weather, or an emergency, we will notify families as early as possible through the Brightwheel app and by phone, and we generally follow local guidance. Planned closures and the two annual staff in-service days are posted at least four weeks ahead in the app. See Hours & Closures for the holiday calendar.

sections.title: Authorized Pickup & Custody
sections.body: For safety, only adults on your child's authorized pickup list may collect your child, and we ask for a photo ID until our staff know them by sight. You can update authorized adults anytime in the app. Unless we have a current court order on file, we cannot deny a legal parent access to their child; please give us copies of any custody or guardianship orders that affect pickup so we can honor them.

sections.title: Confidentiality & Records
sections.body: Your child's records and your family's information are kept confidential and shared only with staff who work directly with your child, or as required by law. We do not discuss one family's information with another, and you may review your child's records by request. Photos or video of your child are used in the app or for program purposes only with your signed media authorization, which you may decline or change at any time.

sections.title: Child Safety & Mandated Reporting
sections.body: Every child's safety is our first responsibility. All of our staff are trained mandated reporters and are required by law to report any suspected child abuse or neglect to the proper authorities, wherever the concern arises. If you ever have a safety concern, please speak with Director Maria Delgado at (505) 555-0142. In any immediate danger, call 911 first.

sections.title: Inclusion & Children With Additional Needs
sections.body: We welcome children of all abilities and work to include every child fully. If your child has an IFSP, IEP, allergy action plan, or other care plan, please share it so we can make reasonable accommodations and coordinate with your providers. We partner with families early and often, and we will talk with you directly if we think additional support or an evaluation could help your child thrive.

sections.title: Parent Partnership & Concerns
sections.body: Families are partners in their child's learning. You are welcome to visit, volunteer, and join family events, and we share daily updates, photos, and reminders through the Brightwheel app, with conferences each fall and spring. If you have a question, idea, or concern, please start with your child's teacher; if it is not resolved, contact Director Maria Delgado at (505) 555-0142. We take concerns seriously and aim to respond promptly.

sections.title: Field Trips & Neighborhood Walks
sections.body: Older classrooms may take occasional neighborhood walks or field trips that support what children are learning. We send the details and a permission request through the app before any trip off site, and we keep our required adult-to-child ratios and each child's emergency information with us throughout.

lunchMenu.Monday: Turkey and cheese roll-ups, steamed carrots, apple slices, and milk.
lunchMenu.Tuesday: Cheese quesadilla with black beans, brown rice, cucumber rounds, and milk.
lunchMenu.Wednesday: Whole-grain pasta with marinara, roasted zucchini, pear slices, and milk.
lunchMenu.Thursday: Baked chicken, sweet potato wedges, green peas, and milk.
lunchMenu.Friday: Veggie and cheese mini-pizzas, garden salad, orange wedges, and milk.

faqs.question: Do you offer part-time schedules?
faqs.answer: Yes. A three-day-per-week schedule is available at 70% of the full-time monthly rate, subject to openings in your child's age group.

faqs.question: How will I get updates about my child's day?
faqs.answer: We share daily photos, meals, naps, and reminders through the Brightwheel app, and offer parent-teacher conferences each fall and spring.

faqs.question: Is there a sibling discount?
faqs.answer: Yes, we offer a 10% discount on the younger child's tuition when siblings are enrolled at the same time.
